data.body:
G4 (child consistency) — parent epic `5fd2` × child `681b` (ISF criterion: build + validate): CLEAN / no findings.

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the whole sibling roster (high-confidence only; benign-reading filter applied):

1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — ISF's feeding machinery (`_linked_session_log` / `_ticket_graph_blob` / `summarize_for_isf`) lives on the orchestrator surface owned by `ca03`; the sharing is EXPLICITLY coordinated via the stated ownership split (681b owns the criterion+prompt+session-log gating; ca03 owns orchestration/feeding). No uncoordinated shared state. CLEAN.

2. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — examined the strongest candidate: the parent's e6d9-dry-run AGENT-tier ROUTING RULE ("a criterion that needs the broader ticket dependency graph MUST be AGENT-tier, NOT single-turn fed-context") vs ISF being single-turn/2-STEP, fed an INJECTED ticket graph, NOT agent. Benign-reading resolves it: the parent registry names ISF explicitly with this exact tier+feed ("ISF intent-source fidelity (single-turn/2-STEP, FED the linked session log + ticket graph, NOT agent)"), and the AGENT-tier reclassification section enumerates G3/G4 + COH but pointedly does NOT reclassify ISF — the parent carves ISF out BY NAME. `ca03` realizes that carve-out as a PRE-RESOLVED injected blob resolved from already-loaded state (ISF never fetches the graph), not the incomplete snapshot the routing rule warns against. No contradiction. CLEAN.

3. DEPENDENCY GAP — `681b` relates_to `7284`; `7284` explicitly CONSUMES the ISF build and ADDS its eval cases. No missing breaking edge. CLEAN.

4. SCOPE OVERLAP — ISF surfaces in `681b` (criterion+prompt+session-log gating), `7284` (eval cases/harness), and `ca03` (orchestration/feeding); all three state the non-duplicating split, backed by relates_to links. No double-build. CLEAN.

5. ORDERING VIOLATION — consistent order (build → eval → orchestrate-feed); no inversion. CLEAN.

6. CONSUMER IMPACT — ISF emits the shared `plan_review_findings` schema + the `_reduced_confidence` flag and integrates into the existing Pass-1/2/3 pipeline. (The flag's downstream consumption was a within-child T8 completeness note, not a cross-child contradiction.) CLEAN.

7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — all referenced artifacts (ISF finder/summarizer prompts, ISF eval spec, ticket-graph blob) exist; no stale references. CLEAN.

Net: empty findings list. The strongest candidate (AGENT-tier-vs-fed-context) is explicitly reconciled by the parent naming ISF as the carve-out, so flagging it would be a false-fire under the benign-reading filter.
